10318.label: Congregation
10318.value: St John the Evangelist Epis Church

St John’s is located in downtown Stockton, is visible and accessible to the community. Our vision is to embody Christ’s authentic hospitality, as we see our church where every person is welcomed at the table, fully belonging and deeply known. Our dream is to have a clergy person who will take on the liturgical and pastoral responsibility of our church, stimulate our growth, lead us by example, with discernment, consistency, inclusivity, compassion and courage. It is the hope of the Bishop and Diocesan Council that under the guidance of the new Priest-in-Charge, St. John’s will continue to grow our congregation, remain fervent to our ministries, serve our community, and help us move from Mission to Parish status. 

Who we hope is “Called to be” with us… 
--Collaborative: must be “hands on” with our community and our partners such as Uplift All Foundation, and be present when services such as showers, laundry, meals, medical checks, etc are being provided 
--Welcoming: St John’s is a place for everyone, a sanctuary for those struggling with mental health and addiction, the unhoused and forgotten; we are guided by the Spirit to love and serve all 
--Community focused: our message of “love and care for all” travels throughout the city. We are present at Pride events in June and October, we participate in the Run and Walk for the Emergency Food Bank, we aid the work of the Mercy Peddlers, we host fundraising events 
---Entrepreneurial: have big dreams and big ideas for how we can develop our ministries in the heart of downtown Stockton, a priest who is inspiring, creative, energetic and enthusiastic, as well as having financial management experience; we aspire to explore Latino ministry in our context. Our urban location is bisected by the Cinco de Mayo and Our Lady of Guadalupe events. 
--Faithful: this clergy person will need to be able to navigate the complexities of partnership and change while being rooted in their call from God, and they need to be able to share that faith and love with others
Send cover letter, resume, and vocations hub profile along with two video sermons to canonanna@diosanjoaquin.org

It is a 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom home with a den, formal living and dining rooms, sun room, kitchen, basement, garage and carport.  Estimated square footage is 1800 sq feet.  There is an expectation the full-time rector will live at the Rectory.

10346.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10346.value: The new priest should be passionate about Jesus and His teachings.  We want our new priest to be a positive person, one who can motivate and uplift the current congregation to accomplish goals of spiritual and physical growth.  We want our priest to be kind and compassionate to all people and set the example for loving each and every person.  We ask that he/she to be patient with us and our congregation as we learn and grow together.  We require honesty and dependability.  We prefer someone who is hard-working, not afraid to “dig in” and work with the congregation.  We need a critical thinker, one who can help us solve the problems of today and tomorrow.    We are hoping for flexibility and understanding, but a boldness to stand with us as we grow our church.

Zion has complete a 3 year priest-in-charge assignment which helped them shift from a 20+ yr rectorship to an empowered laity. Together they addressed a financial deficit, sold the rectory outside of town and invested the proceeds into renovating the rectory near the church and made a significant contribution to their endowment. The congregation has a significant impact in the community and has built relationships with ecumenical partners in the area. Their next priest has an opportunity to build on the current culture of tranparency, inclusion and collaboration.

Letter, resume and OTM portfolio to Transition@diovermont.org
Canon Linda L Grenz

10353.value:
St. Paul’s:  Who God is calling us to be…
Through a process of discernment and deep listening, we identified our strengths and weaknesses. We believe that God is calling us to be a welcoming, supportive and safe presence in our community. As an Episcopal community, we have experienced a lot of change over the years and have identified that we need a stronger and more visible presence in our community.
The areas we feel God is calling us to pay attention to are:

-Growing as a congregation spiritually, financially, and in the ways in which we engage the surrounding community.
-Furthering our prayer life through a variety of opportunities: bible study, mid-week Eucharist, and formation. We feel these opportunities are a way for us to deepen our faith and welcome others to community prayer life.
-Exploring opportunities to partner with neighboring Episcopal churches in Turlock and Hanford
Send cover letter, resume, vocations hub profile and 2 video sermons to canonanna@diosanjoaquin.org

10353.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10353.value:
Who we hope is “Called to be” with us…

St. Paul’s Visalia is hoping that our new Priest-in-Charge will be:

--Administrative:  have strong technology, communication, organizational, and administrative skills, and also be able to delegate responsibilities to our lay leaders

--Collaborative:  be a good listener who values input from the congregation, willing to “get the lay of the land” rather than immediately making changes, interested in reaching out to the community, supportive of our ministry to the unhoused community in our neighborhood, and supportive of the 12-step programs and other groups who call our campus “home”; bilingual (Spanish) and/or Latino/Hispanic cultural competency is a plus

--A person of deep and abiding faith:  this clergy person will need to be able to navigate the complexities of partnership and change while being rooted in their call from God, who is creative about ways to invite more people to church, including families, and they need to be able to share that faith and love with others 

--Loving:  someone who respects diversity at all levels be it race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, ability, age, and socio-economic status, is compassionate, and is committed to providing pastoral care to our parishioners; this person should also be able to interpret scripture to address our lived experiences and current context

10571.value:
Christ Episcopal Church in Cody, Wyoming, seeks a full-time rector. Cody is a community of about 10,400 people nestled near Yellowstone National Park's East Entrance.  We are a congregation with deep roots in our community, a proud tradition of service and worship, and a genuine hunger to deepen our faith. 

The town is large enough to provide essential services, including Cody Regional Health (a full-service hospital that attracts top medical professionals), three elementary schools, a middle school, a high school, and an alternative high school. Cody also supports a mix of locally owned shops, restaurants, and small businesses alongside larger retailers. Yellowstone Regional Airport is located in Cody and serves all of northwest Wyoming. Cody has a vibrant cultural community including several museums, public library, recreation center with an indoor ice rink, local community theaters, as well as a variety of lively music venues. The community is friendly, safe, and family-oriented. Billings, Montana, is 90 minutes away, offering expanded medical and cultural resources and additional air service. Cody is known for its Western heritage, welcoming atmosphere, and quality of life. Our parish reflects that character: independent, resilient, and deeply connected to the land and to each other. 

We seek a rector who will walk alongside us, challenge us, and guide us toward renewed spiritual growth. We yearn for worship that challenges us, leadership that models discipleship, and guidance in taking the next steps in our walk with God. We offer a community full of warmth and service, and we are eager for a leader who will help us embrace our call to love God and neighbor more fully.

Candidates wishing to apply for the position, please send the following directly to the Search Committee at Christ Church Cody:
A cover letter addressed to the Vestry describing what appeals to you about Christ Church Cody and how you see your gifts and skills matching with theirs.
Vocation Hub profile (in pdf format)
Resume

searchcommittee@christchurchcody.org

For other questions please contact Diocesan Transition Minister Rev. Canon Bobbe Fitzhugh - bobbe@episcopalwy.org

10571.value: 2. How are you preparing yourself for the Church of the future?, 5. What has been your experience leading change in the Church?, 12. Describe a recent ministry experiment or innovation.

10571.label: Location
10571.value:
The Christ Church Budget for calendar year 2026 is $633,573. Revenues to fund this budget come from the following categories: Giving, Restricted Endowment Funds, Unrestricted Endowment Funds, Funds generated by Bargain Box Stores, Building Endowment Fund, and Grant Funds. Expenses match revenue, with operating expenses equal to $443,573, and other expenses to include disbursements for mission, grants, capitol projects, and emergency assistance. 

Christ Church has an unrestricted endowment fund of 2,920,000 and restricted endowment funds of $921,000. With the passing of major donors during recent years and the resultant
decrease in giving, the percentage of funds required from the unrestricted endowment to fulfill the budget has grown. This year’s budget requires a 7% draw from the endowment based on a
12 quarter rolling average of the unrestricted fund.

The monies generated by the operations of the two Bargain Box Stores is used exclusively for mission outreach to the community. This year’s budget anticipates a sprinkler system upgrade
funded from the endowment restricted building fund and the creation of a prayer garden which will be funded from a grant from the Foundation for the Episcopal Diocese of Wyoming.

Mileage to be paid at the IRS rate for mileage reimbursement.

10571.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10571.value:
Future leaders of our Episcopal worshipping community must embody a vibrant blend of strong authentic faith, which they draw upon in everyday life.  We are looking for a dynamic leader with faith to guide, model and teach us how to grow in love of God and neighbor.  Ideally, this would be someone who is charismatic and enthusiastic in the pulpit--able to inspire with both word and presence.  He/she would be a true shepherd, drawing people in, sparking renewed interest and commitment  to life in the church.  More specifically,  nurturing our youth programs to ignite young spirits as well as exploring intergenerational worship for life-long learning.  We hope for an individual gifted in pastoral counseling with a commitment to developing deep relationships with parishioners.

We envision someone who builds community with warmth, authenticity and strategic planning, helping individuals discover their unique gifts and guiding them to grow in service and spirit.  Their passion will radiate beyond our walls by developing relationships within the broader Cody community.  Most importantly, we desire a deeply spiritual person whose love of God shines through all they do, energizing our parish with faith, purpose and hope.

10571.label: Ministry skills  (maximum of 10)
10571.value: Adult Formation, Children & Family Formation, Innovative Ministry, Pastoral Care, Prayer & Spirituality, Scripture Study, Worship & Liturgy

10598.value:
The parish of St. Matthew’s seeks a multifaceted spiritual leader who leads with humility and love while navigating the administrative realities of an active, program-centered parish. We value servant leadership—leading through service rather than authority—and the ability to collaborate effectively with lay leaders and staff. We hope to call an energetic rector who intends to make St. Matthew’s and Hillsborough their home rather than a temporary stop.
Like many congregations, St. Matthew’s has an aging membership, which creates significant pastoral care needs. While we have strong lay leadership supporting pastoral care ministries, there remains a deep desire for personal pastoral presence from the rector through visits, spiritual counsel, and relational connection. The next rector will need to balance delegation with maintaining meaningful pastoral relationships. We also hope for a priest who can help us continue to welcome and engage younger adults and families. A sense of humor and the ability to connect across generations are highly valued.
St. Matthew’s is also seeking a capable organizational leader who is comfortable with administrative responsibilities. Our rector should be technologically proficient and able to navigate Zoom worship and other digital communication platforms. Because we value continuity in ministry, we hope for a collaborative team player who will work closely with our associate rector and staff, taking time to understand the parish before initiating major changes.
We are an engaged and thoughtful worshiping community that places great importance on preaching. We seek a preacher who centers the Gospel while remaining attentive to the wider context of Christian morality, our parish’s history of enslavement and racial injustice, and the realities of contemporary life. Our next rector should articulate a hopeful vision for the parish and communicate that vision clearly through preaching, teaching, and conversation.
Finally, we seek a rector with emotional maturity who can navigate stress, conflict, and differing perspectives with wisdom and grace. Our parish includes a variety of backgrounds and viewpoints, and we value leaders who foster healthy dialogue while maintaining clear expectations and boundaries when needed. Above all, we hope for a rector who sees every person as precious and worthy of care, modeling that spirit of compassion for the entire community.
Resume, cover letter (addressed to Search Committee), OTM or Vocation Hub profile uploaded here: https://episdionc.formstack.com/forms/applications

10600.value:
The St. Michael’s rectory, two blocks from the church, is a 4-bedroom, 3-bath single-family home built in 1939. The rectory sits on a quiet corner in a lovely neighborhood lined with mature trees. Inside features a newly renovated kitchen and a charming living room with a wood-burning fireplace. A large deck and adjoining screened-in porch provide a lovely space to relax and entertain. In addition to the new kitchen, recent renovations include a new roof, tree maintenance, and a new storage shed.
Residency in the rectory is optional, though most rectors have lived there.

10614.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10614.value:
As Trinity looks to the future, we pray for a collaborative leader who values lay leadership and shared ministry.  The congregation identified four steady gifts they desire, qualities that feel less like a “job description” and more like the kind of shepherd a parish family can trust.

First, trustworthy integrity: someone ethical, respectful, and confidential, who leads with transparency and earns confidence through quiet consistency. Second, genuine connection: a Rector who listens well, communicates clearly, and builds relationships beyond the sermon, someone comfortable with people of every age, including our acolytes and youth. Third, spiritual depth with real-world wisdom: a leader grounded in theology who can preach with relevance and efficiency, and who understands the secular world while helping us apply faith to everyday life, even when situations are not black and white. And fourth, mission-driven leadership: a Rector who honors Trinity’s history in Manassas and Prince William County, respects the strong teams already in place, and guides growth through pastoral care, formation, outreach, music ministry, and traditional liturgy.

Through prayer, conversation, and shared discernment, the Trinity congregation presents this profile as an expression of who we are and the rector we seek. Trinity is ready to welcome a rector who will walk with us into the next chapter of faithful, joyful, and Spirit-led ministry.

10627.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10627.value:
Trinity Parish seeks a visionary rector to lead our urban, multicultural and multiracial congregation in Wilmington, Delaware.

We are two locations, four weekend worship services, and two languages—united in faith, liturgy, and service. Our historic mother church, Old Swedes, anchors our spiritual heritage, while Trinity Church serves as our primary location with three Sunday services and a thriving Spanish-language community.

Who We Are: A welcoming parish celebrating diversity across many ages, cultures, races, and economic backgrounds. We embrace all people—regardless of ability, gender identity, sexual orientation, family structure, or nationality—who seek God's love.

What We Seek: A lively, engaging preacher who connects scripture to contemporary issues and our lives. A leader who will:
•	Strengthen Latino participation in parish leadership and ministries
•	Champion compassionate social justice and community outreach
•	Develop programs for children, young people, and young families
•	Foster collaboration between our Anglo and Latino congregations
•	Support stewardship of Old Swedes and partnerships with the National Parks Service

Our Location: The Wilmington area offers a wealth of activities and historic sites, making it a vibrant place to live and worship. Major urban hubs, beautiful beaches, and picturesque mountains are all within a two-hour drive.

10665.value: St. Peter’s seeks a rector to join our parish family—someone who will lead with faith, joy, and compassion while honoring the strong Episcopal identity and traditions that shape our common life. We desire a priest who is spiritually grounded, pastorally attentive, and relationally engaged: a leader who genuinely enjoys being with people and who inspires confidence and hope for the future of the church. This role is understood as a shared ministry, in which the rector, vestry, and congregation work collaboratively to support one another and advance the mission of the parish.

Applicants, please send your résumé, vocation hub profile, and a letter of introduction and interest to The Rev. Canon Brad Hinton, Canon to the Ordinary: bhinton@delaware.church. Reach out if you would like to discuss this ministry opportunity.

What theological convictions shape your ministry?, 12. Describe a recent ministry experiment or innovation., 15. How do you take care of your well-being-spiritually, emotionally, and physically?

10665.label: Location
10665.value: Through careful stewardship, transparent leadership, and generous participation by parishioners, the congregation has navigated recent clergy transitions while sustaining ministry vitality and financial health. After closing 2024 at break-even, the parish ended 2025 with a strong operating surplus, offering flexibility and opportunity for the next Priest-in-Charge to lead with vision and confidence. Pledge and plate contributions account for nearly half of annual income. The 2025 budget surplus was made possible through the use of supply clergy in the transition period.

10665.label: Range
10665.value: The target salary is $80,000 and is negotiable.

10665.label: Type of Housing Provided
10665.value: Housing allowance is a part of the $80,000 compensation.

10665.label: Vacation & Leave Details
10665.value: Up to 2 weeks and $1,000 per year

10665.label: Vacation & Leave Details
10665.value: Available after the fifth year. No terminal sabbaticals allowed.

10665.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10665.value:
The rector will serve as the parish’s spiritual leader, guiding worship and preaching that is reverent, accessible, and rooted in scripture. Sermons are expected to be engaging, concise, and relatable, helping parishioners connect biblical teaching to their daily lives while consistently affirming God’s unconditional love and grace. A strong understanding of Episcopal liturgy and sacramental life is essential, as is a deep respect for the traditions that define St. Peter’s worship. At the same time, the parish welcomes thoughtful enrichment of worship that speaks to a broad range of ages without compromising its core identity.

Pastoral care is a central expectation of this ministry as is the task of equipping and encouraging lay leaders to share in the ministry of caring. Strong relationships and community engagement are essential to the health of the parish. The rector should be visible, approachable, and able to connect easily with children, youth, adults, and seniors alike. A warm personality, genuine empathy, and a sense of humor are highly valued. The parish hopes for a Rector who actively supports parish ministries, including collaboration with the preschool, and who fosters a welcoming, joyful atmosphere in which all feel accepted, valued, and encouraged to grow in faith.

10665.value: Church Growth, Community Building, Formation, Parish Day School, Pastoral Care, Preaching, Worship & Liturgy

10669.value:
Cople Parish is a warm and loving Body of Christ devoted to a shared love of the teachings of the Gospel. A sense of community is at the forefront of what makes Cople Parish home for our congregants and we are actively focused on growing in that way. We are a group of people who walk into our worship spaces happy to be there. 
Established in 1664, our Parish has three historic church buildings: St. James and Yeocomico, which have services most Sundays, and Nomini, which hosts occasional services. We have one Vestry and one budget. Though many parishioners worship at one church or the other, what brings us all together is our shared intention to live out the Great Commandment. 
Each of the church buildings in our Parish stand in pastoral settings overlooking our local waterways, farm fields and woodlands. The peacefulness of these settings, looking upon the water from St. James’ and into the woods and fields at Yeocomico, reminds us of the beauty God has bestowed upon the earth. Our parishioners feel that there are strength and comfort to be found for all who attend and visit our Parish in these rural spaces and we enjoy sharing them with the broader community. 

We are seeking a Rector who:  
1. Can provide pastoral care to and connect with people in all seasons of life, from children to the elderly. 
2. Has strong spiritual and preaching/teaching skills, able to give sermons that people can understand and apply to their own lives.
3. Has the necessary skills and will be happy to help us develop a vision, mission, and strategy for how to steward our three historic church buildings and two congregations in a changing world while becoming a more tight-knit community.  We endeavor to find a new season of ministry where we all work together to develop a vibrant future for Cople Parish.
4. Can help us learn how to develop and deepen broader community relationships and invite more organizations, people, and events in our spaces.

To apply, please email a letter expressing interest, OTM profile/Vocation Hub profile (if either is available), and resume to the Contact below:

Questions may be forwarded to the Rev. Will Dickinson, Diocesan Transition Minister, at wdickinson@episcopalvirginia.org

10669.label: Location
10669.value: Since 1907, the community that cares about Cople has expressed that by establishing funds that contribute to the parish’s welfare. Our various funds have an overall value of $3.6 mil. Some are restricted to using a yearly distribution only and for a few we can use the principal if necessary. We are also fortunate to have a group of experts in the congregation to help maintain, support, and make use of these funds.

10669.value: Housing Allowance as part of Salary (included in above number)

10669.label: Reimbursement Offered
10669.value: In compliance with CPG (included in above number)

10669.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10669.value:
Cople Parish faces challenges that are not unusual for a rural parish community. Like many churches we have struggled with attendance due to an aging population (the median age in our county is 50). However, our area is attractive to retirees. Those newcomers have been and can continue to be a source of new parishioners. And we do have some very active younger members. The members of our congregation are dedicated to the working of the church however we do not have a deep bench regarding leadership and some members are taking on multiple responsibilities.

Our outreach efforts significantly serve our neighbors, but they don’t often draw folks into our parish. We hope our incoming Rector will be able to help us learn to make deeper connections in the local community and think of new ways to invite folks into our spaces and to help us develop leaders for the next generation.

 St. James’ was built in the 1890s as an ecumenical mission chapel for the then remote village of Tidwells (a very small and isolated village populated by watermen on Lower Machodoc Creek, close to the Potomac River). Nomini Church, on the banks of Nomini Creek and the third church that bears this name, was built in the mid-nineteenth century. The original Yeocomico Church was built as a chapel-of-ease in 1655. It is believed that that original wooden structure is within the walls of the 1706 brick structure that stands today. Yeocomico Church is one of the oldest active places of worship in the United States spanning over 370 years of history. With a Declaration of Independence signer having been a member of our parish, it reminds us of the importance of our past. We embrace the responsibility of the stewardship of our historic churches, but we understand and experience that having three churches also poses a challenge when decisions are made regarding where to hold services and other gatherings. Some parishioners identify with one church building over another. These are issues we are willing to take an honest look at for the betterment of our whole parish family.

We are looking for a new Rector who can help us to develop clear and concise vision and mission statements and produce a strategic plan that will help us to determine the best use of our many buildings for a new season of ministry. We believe this will help encourage our members to continue their good work while also bringing new people into the fold.

10671.value:
Holy Cross Episcopal Church in Hixson, TN, just outside of Chattanooga, is looking for our next Priest-in-Charge! Hixson is a rapidly growing suburban area just outside of Chattanooga, and we are looking to grow with our community.
We are looking for a spiritual leader, who also plans to join us as a partner in ministry. Our culture is one of collegiality—we want you to bring your values and to appreciate ours. The next Priest-in-Charge of Holy Cross will find people willing to serve and looking for guidance and motivation in how best to do so. As one of our Vestry members put it, “There are a lot of big ‘Christ hearts’ in this parish. What we need is someone to help direct them.”
We recently completed a congregation-wide conversation geared toward creating our new mission statement: “Rooted in the love of Christ, we are a welcoming, inclusive, and joyful community where all belong. Through worship, service, and fellowship, we embrace tradition with a progressive spirit—striving always to love our neighbors as ourselves.”
Holy Cross is the newest parish in the Diocese of East Tennessee. Our parish was formed out of the merger of two existing parishes in the Chattanooga area. As such, our “DNA” contains elements of each of those parishes, as well as a strand that is uniquely us--who we are now, rooted in the present and looking toward the future.

If you feel that you may be called to serve as Priest-in-Charge of Holy Cross, please submit a letter of interest, along with your resume or CV, a copy of your OTM portfolio, and recordings of two recent sermons to the Rev. Canon Joe Woodfin, jwoodfin@dioet.org.

10674.value: Our next rector will be a spiritually grounded and prayerful leader—compassionate, courageous, and deeply committed to inclusivity. They will listen attentively, offer wise personal counsel, and cultivate a collaborative environment that inspires and empowers others to grow in their ministries. A deep appreciation for music and its role in liturgy is essential, as music is central to how we express and experience our faith. In a vibrant and engaged parish, strong administrative and time management skills, paired with the ability to delegate and a healthy sense of humor, will be invaluable. Experience in nurturing and expanding youth involvement would also be an asset. The ideal candidate will preach sermons that are authentic, relatable, and rooted in Scripture—messages that connect the Gospel to everyday life, inspire reflection and action, challenge the listener, and include moments of humor and lasting encouragement.
Resume, Cover letter addressed to Search Committee, OTM or Vocation Hub profile uploaded to:  https://episdionc.formstack.com/forms/applications

10683.label: Location
10683.value:
Thanks to strong financial controls and careful budgeting, St. Andrew’s is in a good financial position. We generally come very close to meeting or exceeding the full amount pledged over the course of the year. This was true in 2023 and 2024. For 2025, fulfillment was 94% of $412,344 pledged. Annual expenses are largely fixed, including clergy and staff salaries and benefits, diocesan assessment, insurance, utilities, building maintenance, administrative expenses, and program costs. We finished 2025 with a surplus, and the 2026 budget projects a small deficit. 

Two recent significant contributions to our financial base, a generous gift and the sale of an adjacent property, along with $480,000 in endowments provide breathing room and further opportunities to live out our mission to welcome others to our community and to follow Christ’s example of service to each other and our community.   

St. Andrew’s has no mortgages or liens. We recently completed a campus renovation project with a $1.5 million capital campaign.

10683.value: Negotiable, in compliance with diocese guidelines.

10683.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10683.value:
St. Andrew’s looks forward to a strong and loving relationship with our new rector. We have
identified important areas that a candidate ideally would provide.
1. Inspire us spiritually and bring energy to the congregation through preaching in ways
that make us think, telling us stories and helping us connect the Gospel and scriptures to
our daily lives; meeting with parishioners for spiritual guidance and encouragement; and
supporting our traditional music ministry. The new rector will continue to sustain our
thoughtful use of liturgy, helping us develop ways to be our best selves. We seek to love
God and love our neighbors as ourselves, living out our Baptismal Covenant.
2. The new rector will help us explore and implement ways to grow our congregation. We
can invite people in, reconnect with the St. Francis House Episcopal Student Center
adjacent to the UW-Madison campus, with whom we have had a long-standing
relationship, and provide activities that invite our neighbors in. We are open to new and
innovative ideas. In order to include more youth, we need to consider activities that suit
their needs and interests. Additionally, our community comprises members who attend
one Sunday service exclusively, often without much opportunity to interact with those
from the other service. We seek ways to establish personal connections between these

2
two groups through parish activities, including adult formation activities between
services and at other times of the week, outreach and other projects.
3. The rector will be a servant leader, helping St. Andrew’s grow and develop our lay
ministry. The rector will help us explore, identify, and document our talents, gifts, and
skills as individuals and as a parish community. Although our hearts are willing and
eager, we need assistance to further develop and foster leadership of our lay ministries.
More parish participation would imbue these ministries with vitality. The rector will help
us direct our gifts, talents, and skills toward needs within the parish and in the broader
community, doing the work God sends us to do.
4. The rector will have leadership strength and a sense of humor. They will oversee
administration and help us remain in compliance with all Diocesan policies. They will
empower and support the staff, vestry, and congregation to be engaged in lay
ministries, freeing the rector to use their time within the parish and out in community.

10689.value: The Episcopal Church of the Good Shepherd is a historic and beloved parish in Maitland, FL, with roots going back to 1883. Our property is a significant asset, encompassing our cherished historic chapel, a spacious Sanctuary built in 1967, a Parish Hall, and several outbuildings currently leased to the Celandine Life Prep Academy, a special needs school that brings meaningful weekday activity and community presence to our campus. Maitland is a thriving suburban community of approximately 20,000 residents located within the greater Orlando area, and it is experiencing steady, ongoing growth. That growth is reflected in new apartment construction near our church, bringing with it an influx of younger residents — exactly the demographic a vital, forward-looking parish seeks to engage. We see this as a genuine and exciting opportunity. Good Shepherd is beginning to take intentional steps to reach out to these newer neighbors, and we are eager for a Rector who shares our vision and will help lead that effort. While we are located in an area served by several churches, including two other Episcopal congregations within a few miles, we believe our history and our welcoming community give us a compelling foundation from which to grow. We are looking for a Rector who is energized by opportunity — someone ready to build on what we have and help shape what Good Shepherd will become.

Please contact Canon Ellis Brust, Canon for Clergy in Transition, for more information regarding this position. 
Email: ebrust@cfdiocese.org
Phone: 772-302-9661

10689.label: Range
10689.value: Cash amount includes stipend, housing and SECA offset

10689.label: Pension Plan
10689.value: Pension Plan in compliance with CPF requirements. Healthcare Options are negotiable.

10689.label: Vacation & Leave Details
10689.value: As outlined in the Letter of Agreement. 1 month for each year of service, with maximum of 6 months

10689.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
10689.value: The top request from our congregation regarding our new Rector is someone who is effective at preaching and enjoys this aspect of their ministry. We hope for a Rector who can regularly share the Gospel in a relatable way, so our members can learn how their lives fit into God’s loving plan for His church and His people and join together to accomplish God’s plan for Good Shepherd and our surrounding community. Next, but still highly requested, we are seeking a visionary strategic leader — someone who can look at this parish in this neighborhood and help us see a compelling future. We are looking for a Rector who can help us develop a long-term vision and strategy for Good Shepherd and communicate it in a way that brings our membership along — inspiring ownership, commitment, and shared momentum. We are hoping for someone with skill in outreach and member development — particularly the ability to attract and retain young families. The younger residents moving into the apartments surrounding our campus represent a real opportunity, and we are looking for a Rector who is energized by that opportunity. Experience in connecting with younger demographics, understanding what draws young families to a parish, and helping us build the programming and relationships that keep them engaged will be an important skill. We are looking for someone with a pastor's heart — attentive to the spiritual, emotional, and physical wellbeing of our members and committed to the kind of personal care that has been central to Good Shepherd's identity. Our congregation is a family, and we hope our next Rector can genuinely love and tend that family even as they lead it forward. We hope our next rector would be comfortable speaking to a congregation having a broad theological understanding — someone who can respect our close-knit, community while gently and skillfully expanding our openness to change. This requires theological range and also some amount of relational intelligence and empathy. Finally, we are looking for a leader with the wisdom to guide adaptive change — someone who understands that moving a community forward requires patience, honesty, and skill, not just enthusiasm. This next chapter calls for a leader who has the courage and creativity to help us build what comes next. We are looking for a Rector who will find in Good Shepherd a community eager to grow, and who will consider it a privilege to help us do so.
